text: Peak endurance performance is maintained until approximately age 35, followed by modest decreases until age 50-60, with progressively steeper declines thereafter.
specific_value: maintained until ~35, modest decline 35-60, steep decline after 60

text: A progressive reduction in maximal oxygen consumption (VO2max) is the primary physiological mechanism responsible for declines in endurance performance with age.
A reduction in lactate threshold contributes to age-related endurance performance decline, though this may be secondary to decreases in VO2max.
Exercise economy (metabolic cost of sustained submaximal exercise) does not change with age in endurance-trained adults.
Declines in maximal stroke volume, maximal heart rate, and arterio-venous oxygen difference all contribute to age-related reductions in VO2max in endurance-trained athletes.
Age-related declines in endurance performance are mediated in large part by reductions in training intensity (velocity) and training volume that can be sustained.
